title: Smoky Japanese Fennel Noodles
description: A charred and crumbly Japanese dish featuring fennel bulb, perfect for midnight cravings, with a low-fat friendly twist.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the hokkaido noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium-high heat until it starts to smoke.
Add the sliced fennel to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until it is charred and caramelized.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, grated ginger, and minced garlic.
Add the cooked noodles to the skillet with the fennel and pour the soy sauce mixture over the top.
Stir everything together to combine and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Season the noodles with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the noodles to a serving dish and top with chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds.
